manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: hypersensitivity, demineralization, plantain, chiding, seacoast, denaturalization, undercapitalization, cardiograph, nextdoor.
Your opponent's team words are: musicologist, nearsightedness, playsuit, horsewoman, geophysics, newsbeat, secession, shortlist.
Distractor words are: gingersnap, quadrillion, perpetration, unacceptability, neurophysiologist, tonsillitis, demander.
The kill words are: timekeeping.

message.reasoning:
We need to provide a clue word that relates to some of our team's words, and list which targets. Must be single word, not any of the words.

Our team words: hypersensitivity, demineralization, plantain, chiding, seacoast, denaturalization, undercapitalization, cardiogram? Actually cardiograph, nextdoor.

Goal: maximize number guessed but ensure clue is not ambiguous causing opponent words or distractors.

We must ensure clue not part of any word.

We can choose maybe 2-3 words.

We need to think of a clue linking maybe "plant" but not "plantain" alone? Could choose "banana"? Plantain related to banana, but also "seacoast" no. "hypersensitivity" maybe "allergy". "demineralization" maybe "decalcification". "denaturalization" maybe "remediation"? "undercapitalization" maybe "financing". "chiding" maybe "scolding". "cardiograph" maybe "ECG". "nextdoor" maybe "neighbor". Maybe clue "neighbor" would hint nextdoor but also "seacoast"? Not.

Better to give clue "marine" to link seacoast? Only one word.

Maybe we can aim for 3 words: "banana" clue relates to plantain (both are banana family). Also "hypersensitivity" maybe "allergy" also banana allergy? Not good.

Maybe "decalcify" clue for demineralization, denaturalization? Not.

We need to ensure opponent/distractor words not also related.

We could give clue "financial" linking undercapitalization (lack of capital) and maybe demineralization? Not.

Clue "heart" linking cardiograph and maybe hypersensitivity? Not.

Clue "coast" linking seacoast only.

We might give clue "scold" linking chiding. But only one.

Better to give clue "ECG" but that's abbreviation maybe not allowed? The clue must be a single word; "ECG" is abbreviation, but maybe okay. But also could be considered part of cardiograph? That's the same concept, might be too direct but allowed as clue is not the same word.

But could also be associated with "hypersensitivity" (cardiac sensitivity). Not.

We want to give multiple target words, ensure safe.

Potential clue "budget" relates to undercapitalization (lack of funds) and maybe demineralization? Not.

Possible clue "soil" relates to plantain (grows), demineralization (soil mineral loss), denaturalization (removal of natural state), maybe seacoast (coastal soil). But risk: "soil" also could hint "nearsightedness"? not.

Distractors: "quadrillion", "gingersnap". Not.

Opponent words: "geophysics" deals with soil, could be confused. That's opponent word, we must avoid ambiguous clue that could lead to opponent's "geophysics". If we give clue "soil", partner might think geophysics (soil physics). That would be a mistake. So avoid.

Clue "banana"? Could opponent guess "playsuit"? No. Distractors not relevant. So safe.

Plantain is banana relative. Also "hypersensitivity" - banana allergy? Might be too obscure. But partner might guess only plantain. That's okay.

We can add also "nextdoor" clue "neighbor". That's straightforward. Also could also hint opponent "neighbor"? Not present.

Thus we can give two clues separate turns, but we must output only one clue now. Choose a clue that covers at least two of our words. Maybe "neighbor" covers nextdoor and also "seacoast"? Not.

Maybe "coast" covers seacoast and plantain? No.

Maybe "decalcify" covers demineralization and denaturalization? Hmm.

Denaturalization is the process of making unnatural, similar to demineralization? Both have "de-". Could clue "removal" link denaturalization and demineralization maybe also undercapitalization (removing capital). Could be ambiguous with opponent "shortlist"? Not.

But "removal" might also hint "chiding"? No.

I think safest is give clue "banana" targeting plantain only - but we want multiple.

Clue "heart" for cardiograph and hypersensitivity (cardiac sensitivity). Could also hint opponent "neurophysiologist"? Not.

But "heart" maybe opponent "neurophysiologist"? No.

Will partner guess cardiograph for heart, maybe also hypersensitivity? Might think "allergy" though.

Maybe clue "ECG" is directly cardiograph, but also could be seen as abbreviation for "electrocardiogram". Could also hint "hypersensitivity"? Not.

Given risk, we can give clue "finance" for undercapitalization. Only one though.

Better to give two-word clue? Not allowed.

Probably give clue "banana" and target plantain. Then later we can give other clues.

Thus output:

CLUE: banana
TARGETS: plantain

But the prompt wants us to give one clue that relates to some of our team's words. That's fine.

We must ensure clue is not one of the list words: banana is not. Good.

Thus answer
